description: LLM-powered self-assessment generator for performance review season. Aggregates a quarter (or custom date range) of git activity, PR data, and review contributions; extracts themes from commit messages; computes time invested; feeds structured context to LLM; streams a markdown-formatted self-assessment to stdout.

userStories.title: Quarter date-range helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want blackbox to automatically compute the current calendar quarter's date range so I don't have to pass --from/--to manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `quarter_range() -> (DateTime<Utc>, DateTime<Utc>)` in `query.rs` alongside `today_range`, `week_range`, `month_range`.
Q1=Jan-Mar, Q2=Apr-Jun, Q3=Jul-Sep, Q4=Oct-Dec. Range is [quarter_start_midnight_local, now_utc].
Unit test for each quarter boundary.

userStories.title: Custom date-range flag
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `blackbox perf-review --from 2025-01-01 --to 2025-03-31` so I can generate reviews for past quarters or non-standard periods.
acceptanceCriteria: `PerfReview` command in `cli.rs` accepts `--from <YYYY-MM-DD>` and `--to <YYYY-MM-DD>` optional args.
When omitted, defaults to current calendar quarter via `quarter_range()`.
Invalid date strings produce a clear error via `anyhow::bail!`.
Dates are parsed in local timezone, converted to UTC for DB queries.

userStories.title: Quarterly activity aggregation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the command to aggregate all commits, branch names, and time estimates across the date range so the LLM has complete raw material.
acceptanceCriteria: Reuses existing `query_activity(conn, from, to, ...)` — no new DB schema needed.
Calls `enrich_with_prs` for PR data (graceful degradation if gh unavailable).
Computes `global_estimated_time` for total hours invested.
Populates `ActivitySummary` with `period_label` set to e.g. "Q1 2025" or "Jan 1 – Mar 31 2025".

userStories.title: Commit-message theme extraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the system to identify recurring themes (feature areas, tech domains) from commit messages before sending to the LLM, so the prompt is richer and token usage stays bounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `extract_commit_themes(repos: &[RepoSummary]) -> Vec<String>` in a new `perf_review.rs` module.
Extracts the top-N (default 20) most-frequent lowercase word stems from all commit messages, excluding stop-words (fix, the, a, an, add, update, chore, feat, refactor, test, wip, merge, bump, etc.).
Returns sorted list of `"theme (N occurrences)"` strings.
If total commits < 5, returns empty vec without error.
Unit tests: empty input, all stop-words, normal corpus.

userStories.title: PR summary compilation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a structured PR summary (authored PRs and reviewed PRs) included in the LLM context so the review mentions cross-team collaboration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `compile_pr_summary(repos: &[RepoSummary]) -> PrSummary` in `perf_review.rs`.
`PrSummary` struct contains: `authored_prs: Vec<(repo_name, pr_number, pr_title)>` (from `pr_info`), `reviewed_prs: Vec<(repo_name, pr_number, pr_title, action)>` (from `reviews`).
Deduplicates reviewed PRs by pr_number per repo (keep highest-priority action: APPROVED > CHANGES_REQUESTED > COMMENTED).
If gh unavailable, both vecs are empty — no error.
Unit tests for dedup logic.

userStories.title: Perf-review LLM prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a purpose-built system prompt and structured user message that instructs the LLM to write a performance review self-assessment, not just a daily summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `PERF_REVIEW_SYSTEM_PROMPT: &str` in `llm.rs` (or `perf_review.rs`). Prompt instructs: write in first-person professional tone, ~400–600 words, markdown with sections: Summary, Key Contributions, Technical Themes, Collaboration, Time Investment.

Add `generate_perf_review(config: &LlmConfig, context: &PerfReviewContext) -> anyhow::Result<()>` that serializes context to JSON and streams via existing `stream_anthropic`/`stream_openai` infrastructure.
`max_tokens` set to 2048 (reviews are longer than daily summaries).
If total_commits == 0 and total_reviews == 0, return `Err` with message: "No activity found for this period. Is the daemon running?"
Token budget guard: if serialized context > 40,000 chars, truncate commit message list to most-recent 200 commits with a note appended to the prompt.

userStories.title: PerfReviewContext struct
userStories.description: As an implementer, I need a typed context struct that bundles all data for the LLM so serialization is deterministic and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `PerfReviewContext` in `perf_review.rs` with fields: `period_label: String`, `total_commits: usize`, `total_reviews: usize`, `total_repos: usize`, `total_estimated_hours: f64`, `total_ai_session_hours: f64`, `repos: Vec<PerfReviewRepo>`, `themes: Vec<String>`, `pr_summary: PrSummary`.
`PerfReviewRepo` contains: `repo_name`, `commits`, `branches`, `estimated_hours`, `recent_commit_messages: Vec<String>` (max 50 per repo), `authored_prs`, `reviewed_prs`.
Derives `serde::Serialize`.
Add `build_perf_review_context(summary: &ActivitySummary) -> PerfReviewContext` that assembles all the above.
Unit test: round-trips through serde_json.

userStories.title: CLI command: `blackbox perf-review`
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `blackbox perf-review` and get a streamed markdown self-assessment.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `PerfReview { from: Option<String>, to: Option<String> }` variant to `Commands` enum in `cli.rs`.
Add match arm in `main.rs` that: loads config, opens DB, resolves date range, calls `query_activity`, calls `enrich_with_prs`, builds `PerfReviewContext`, calls `generate_perf_review`.
If `llm_api_key` not configured, print helpful error (same pattern as `build_llm_config`).
Command is NOT added to `is_exempt_from_config_check`.
`cargo build` succeeds. `blackbox perf-review --help` shows meaningful description.

userStories.title: Sparse activity graceful handling
userStories.description: As a developer with < 1 month of blackbox data, I want the command to still work and note the limited data window rather than crash or produce nonsense.
acceptanceCriteria: If total_commits < 10, prepend a warning line to the LLM prompt: "Note: limited data — only {n} commits recorded. User may have started tracking recently."
If zero commits AND zero reviews: return error "No activity found for this period." before making any LLM call.
Integration test (with fake DB): verifies error returned for empty period.

userStories.title: No-API-key error UX
userStories.description: As a developer who hasn't configured an LLM key, I want a clear actionable error message explaining exactly what to add to config.toml.
acceptanceCriteria: Error message references `perf-review` specifically: "blackbox perf-review requires an LLM API key..."
Shows exact TOML snippet to add.
Reuses `build_llm_config` error path — no extra code needed beyond ensuring the command calls it before doing any DB work that could be slow.

userStories.title: New module `perf_review.rs`
userStories.description: As an implementer, I want all perf-review logic isolated in its own module so it doesn't pollute query.rs or llm.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/perf_review.rs` with `PerfReviewContext`, `PerfReviewRepo`, `PrSummary`, `build_perf_review_context`, `extract_commit_themes`, `compile_pr_summary`, `generate_perf_review`, `PERF_REVIEW_SYSTEM_PROMPT`.
Declare `pub mod perf_review;` in `lib.rs`.
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes on the new module.

userStories.title: Integration test: perf-review CLI with mock DB
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want an integration test that exercises the full perf-review path (minus actual LLM call) so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `tests/perf_review.rs`.
Test: create temp DB, insert 15 commits across 2 repos + 3 reviews, run `blackbox perf-review` with no LLM key → expect exit code 1 and stderr contains "API key".
Test: `--from 2020-01-01 --to 2020-01-01` (empty range) → exit 1, stderr contains "No activity".
Test: `--from baddate` → exit 1, stderr contains "Invalid date".
Uses `assert_cmd::Command`, `tempfile::TempDir`.

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
